# Figure 5.8 — Soft-DTW Growth Function, $k = m = 3$

**Solver:** Soft-DTW gradient descent (`soft_dtw_balls` submodule)  
**What was fixed:** $k = m = 3$ (thesis notation)  
**What varies:** sample size $s$  
**Canonical source:** `soft_dtw_balls/growth_function_results.csv`  
**Generated by:** `soft_dtw_balls/growth_function_estimation.ipynb`

## File

`growth_function_results.csv` — one row per $(s, \text{sample})$ combination,
aggregated across multiple random point sets.

## CSV schema

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `s` | Sample size (number of query points tested) |
| `k`, `m` | Code convention (swap applies; for $k=m=3$ values are the same) |
| `2^s` | Maximum achievable count $2^s$ |
| `pi_hat` | Estimated growth function $\Pi(s)$ — max shattered subsets across sampled sets |
| `mean_count`, `std_count`, `min_count` | Statistics over sampled point sets |
| `frac_max` | `pi_hat / 2^s` — fraction of all subsets realised |
| `fully_shattered_any` | Whether any sample achieved full $2^s$ shattering |
| `all_counts` | JSON list: per-sample shattering counts |
| `gamma`, `epochs`, `retries` | Soft-DTW optimisation hyperparameters |
| `sampling_mode`, `sampling_radius_noise`, `num_samples` | Point-sampling config |
| `timestamp` | ISO timestamp of the run |

## How this data was generated

```bash
cd soft_dtw_balls
python run_growth_function.py --k 3 --m 3 --s_max 9 --num_samples 20
```
(code notation: `--k 3` = thesis $m=3$; `--m 3` = thesis $k=3$; diagonal so values match)

Or interactively via `growth_function_estimation.ipynb`.
